---
title: "Applied public-health GIS: health department practicum"
subtitle: "Spatial analysis and surveillance analytics for a local health department"
---

::: {.tags .project-tags}
[Applied epidemiology]{.tag} [ArcGIS]{.tag} [Space-time analysis]{.tag} [Surveillance data]{.tag} [Public health]{.tag}
:::

::: {.callout-note appearance="simple"}
Data-sensitive details are omitted. This describes methods and deliverables at a
level that discloses nothing about individuals or protected data.
:::

## Problem

Small health departments generate plenty of data (case reports, immunization
records, addresses) but rarely have the capacity to turn it into spatial
analysis that could guide where limited resources go. Two concrete needs came up
during my MPH practicum: leadership wanted to know *where* respiratory illness
was clustering across the jurisdiction, and the immunization program needed to
know which patients were overdue for required doses so nurses could follow up.

## Approach

The pattern was the same both times. Take the operational data, clean and
structure it, run the appropriate spatial or record-level analysis, and hand
back something the staff can re-run and act on (a notebook, a map, a report),
with a plain-language note on what it does and doesn't show.

## Methods and tools

**Respiratory illness spatial analysis**

- QA and structuring of a 3,454-record case export (2015–2026) into an
  analyzable dataset.
- A reusable, time-enabled hex-binned count workflow, so the analysis can be
  re-run by changing a single input.
- Space-time cube construction and **emerging hot-spot analysis** to tell
  persistent or intensifying trends apart from one-off spikes.
- Getis-Ord Gi\* hot- and cold-spot analysis by time period.
- A standalone HTML web map and a plain-language method note for non-technical
  staff.
- Built with ArcGIS Pro and `arcpy`.

**Immunization-compliance reporting**

- A specification mapping required vaccines to dose groups and age-tiered
  requirements, including the exception rules.
- Calculated fields for per-group dose counts, missing doses, and school-entry
  compliance status, built on EHR-exported data.
- A missed-dose patient list for outreach, and groundwork for mapping
  vaccine-uptake patterns from geocoded addresses.
- QA and logic-verification steps documented alongside the build. This included
  catching a report-duplication issue in the EHR export that was inflating dose
  counts, and flagging it so the numbers weren't used for outreach before the
  fix.

## Status

The respiratory-illness analysis was completed and handed off, and it was used
operationally to help plan outreach. The immunization-compliance work is in
progress.
